one_time_description: Large deferred revenue adjustment of $15,497,123 in 2022 (vs -$47,905,941 in 2021), related to recognition of previously deferred government transfers including BC Reconciliation Funding and Government of Canada contributions
economic_notes: Band owns Bella Bella Fuel Co., Bella Bella Band Store Ltd., Bella Bella Fisheries Ltd., Heiltsuk Environmental Services Ltd., CMHC Section 95 Housing. GBEs include Heiltsuk Holdings LP, Heiltsuk Holdings Ltd., Widsten Holdings Ltd., and 25% interest in Central Coast Commercial Fisheries LP. Investment of $8,258,634 in Widsten Holdings Ltd. during year. Reconciliation funding transferred to Heiltsuk Fishery Resource LP ($171,500) for fish plant upgrade and Heiltsuk Coastal Forest Products LP ($594,733) for sawmill acquisition.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED because financial information for Heiltsuk Holdings Limited Partnership and Heiltsuk Holdings Ltd. was not available for fiscal 2022 and was not accounted for in the consolidated statements. Large deferred revenue adjustment ($15.5M in 2022 vs -$47.9M in 2021) significantly distorts year-over-year comparability. Prior year (2021) included $40,809,991 from Government of Canada (likely a large settlement or reconciliation receipt) and -$47,905,941 deferred revenue adjustment. BC Reconciliation Funding of $22.33M carried in deferred revenue. No schedule of remuneration disclosed in the extracted text. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($22,988,966) + Gov of Canada ($61,963) + BC Housing ($5,607,935) + DFO ($1,102,456) + Province of BC ($878,303) + CMHC ($616,445) + Great Bear Initiative ($377,222) + FNESC ($95,474) + Dept of Justice Canada ($84,225) + BC FN Gaming ($641,355) - but note BC FN Gaming and some items may be considered OSR; figure is approximate.
